ops.text: Spawned child agents sometimes misread the orchestration brief injected via --append-system-prompt: the cmux re-smoke child visibly mused about "instructions embedded in the thinking content" and hesitated before arming its receive Monitor and complying, and the tmux child got stuck in a self-echo Monitor loop that queued an incoming send-message instead of replying live. The spawnBrief wording (orchestrate/spawn.go) could state more plainly that it IS the orchestrator's legitimate system prompt and that incoming orchestrator messages should be answered as a fresh user turn, not queued behind the monitor.
